 {"id":"bd-12vyy","title":"Task: REST metrics, analytics, context, git endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose metrics/analytics, context packs, and git coordination via REST.\n\n## Endpoints\n- Metrics: /metrics, /metrics/compare, /metrics/export, /metrics/snapshot, /metrics/save, /metrics/list, /analytics.\n- Context: /context/build, /context/{id}, /context/stats, /context/cache (DELETE).\n- Git: /git/sync, /git/status.\n\n## Logging\n- Log snapshot IDs, context pack IDs, and git sync outcomes.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: validation + error codes.\n- Integration: snapshot -> export; context build -> fetch; git status output.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Endpoints mirror CLI/robot functionality.\n- Metrics exports preserve existing semantics + schema.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:50:18.491586359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:05:40.455984091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12vyy","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:05:34.335135465Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12vyy","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:05:40.455934678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","notes":"VHS tape files created in testdata/vhs/. E2E test wrapper in tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go. Visual regression script at scripts/visual-regression.sh. Directories testdata/golden and testdata/screenshots created. Golden images need to be generated when VHS is installed. Infrastructure is complete, ready to generate golden images with: UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test -v -run TestUpdateGoldenImages ./tests/e2e","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:09.085341313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067104553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067005807Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:46.574761055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-3b3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:38.386465128Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-13rct","title":"Task: Implement EarlyStopDetector for marginal utility stopping","description":"# Task: EarlyStopDetector Implementation

Stop ensemble when new findings rate drops below threshold.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
    Enabled             bool
}

type EarlyStopDetector struct {
    Config        EarlyStopConfig
    Outputs       []ModeOutput
    TokensSpent   []int
}

type StopDecision struct {
    ShouldStop    bool
    Reason        string
    FindingsRate  float64
    SimilarityScore float64
    AgentsRun     int
}

func NewEarlyStopDetector(cfg EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopDetector
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) RecordOutput(output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) ShouldStop() StopDecision
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateFindingsRate() float64
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateSimilarity() float64
```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```
Rate = (new unique findings in window) / (tokens spent in window)
Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents
```

### Output Similarity
```
Similarity = average pairwise similarity of outputs in window
Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)
```

## Logging
- Log each decision with metrics
- Log when approaching threshold
- Log final stop reason

// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go

    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first

```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```

Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents

Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)

 {"id":"bd-144k","title":"Fix help/keybinding component to render hints once","description":"## Problem
Keyboard navigation hints ("↑↓ navigate", "1-9 select", "z zoom", etc.) appear multiple times in the dashboard footer area due to content overflow.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple lines showing: "ti navigate 1-9 select z zoom r refresh ? help q quit"

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2084 - renderHelpBar() called
- internal/tui/components/help.go:63-110 - RenderHelpBar implementation

## Analysis
The RenderHelpBar function itself is correctly implemented:
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for width calculations (line 93-101)
- Returns a fresh string each call
- No state accumulation

The duplication occurs because:
1. Help bar is appended to main content without height management
2. When content overflows terminal, old frames with help bars remain visible
3. Same root cause as status bar duplication (bd-2usu)

## Fix Required
This is fixed by implementing proper footer region management (see bd-2usu). The help bar should be:
1. Part of the fixed footer region
2. Rendered after the content area is height-constrained
3. Not included in any scrollable viewport

## Additional Improvements
1. Ensure help hints are context-aware (show relevant hints for focused panel)
2. Add width-gated progressive disclosure (fewer hints on narrow terminals)
3. Consider sticky help bar that always shows essential hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Help hints update based on focused panel
- [ ] Narrow terminals show abbreviated hints
- [ ] No duplication on resize or scroll

## Unit Tests
1. TestHelpBar_RendersOnce - "navigate" appears exactly once in View()
2. TestHelpBar_ContextualHints - hints change based on focusedPanel
3. TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation - narrow width removes rightmost hints
4. TestHelpBar_NoAccumulation - multiple View() calls produce same output

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log number of hints rendered
- DEBUG: Log width truncation decisions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:39.445792941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.382075262Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.381982357Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:34.101492049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:31.520119673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14h3d","title":"Task: Implement FindingsVelocity metric","description":"# Task: FindingsVelocity Implementation

Track insights per token spent for efficiency analysis.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/velocity.go
type VelocityTracker struct {
    Entries []VelocityEntry
}

type VelocityEntry struct {
    ModeID        string
    ModeName      string
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    UniqueFindings int
    Velocity      float64 // findings per 1k tokens
}

type VelocityReport struct {
    Overall        float64 // total unique findings / total tokens * 1000
    PerMode        []VelocityEntry
    HighPerformers []string // modes above average
    LowPerformers  []string // modes below threshold
    Suggestions    []string
}

func NewVelocityTracker() *VelocityTracker
func (v *VelocityTracker) RecordOutput(modeID string, output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (v *VelocityTracker) CalculateVelocity() *VelocityReport
func (v *VelocityTracker) Render() string
```

## Velocity Calculation
```
velocity = (unique_findings / tokens_spent) * 1000

Unique findings are de-duplicated by:
- normalized finding text (lowercase + trim)
- evidence_pointer when present
```

## Inputs
- Token counts from BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator (per-mode)
- Outputs from normalized ModeOutput

## Edge Cases
- tokens_spent == 0 → skip or mark velocity as 0 with warning
- empty findings → velocity 0

## Visualization
```
Findings Velocity:
Overall: 2.3 findings / 1k tokens

Per Mode:
F1 Argument Mapping:    3.1 findings/1k (HIGH)
E2 Root Cause:          2.8 findings/1k
K1 Systems Thinking:    1.2 findings/1k (LOW)
L3 Meta-cognitive:      2.5 findings/1k
H2 Strategic:           1.9 findings/1k

Suggestion: K1 underperforming, consider early stop
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log tokens + findings per mode
- Log threshold crossings (below min velocity)

## Tests
- Unit: unique finding de-duplication
- Unit: zero-token handling
- Unit: per-mode and overall velocity math

 {"id":"bd-14yb4","title":"Task: Web UI beads deck (Kanban + Triage + Galaxy)","description":"## Scope\nImplement Beads deck: Kanban board, triage panel, and dependency graph (galaxy view).\n\n## Implementation\n- Kanban drag‑and‑drop with status updates via REST.\n- Triage panel uses bv triage response (health, recommendations, quick wins).\n- Galaxy view uses insights graph with force layout + legends.\n\n## Logging\n- Client logs for bead status changes (dev only).\n\n## Tests\n- E2E: drag bead to in_progress, verify REST update + WS event.\n- Unit: grouping and layout helpers.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Bead status changes are reflected live.\n- Triage/insights panels render with correct counts.\n","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:52:51.841625497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:10:42.897647559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14yb4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kq4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:10:42.897615578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14yb4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vzaq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:10:31.346040714Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14yb4","depends_on_id":"bd-35i7a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:10:18.914536144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -70,7 +70,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1c6d7","title":"DCG: Implement send command interception for non-Claude agents","description":"Intercept 'ntm send' commands and run through DCG before forwarding to pane.

## Why Needed

Codex and Gemini agents don't have PreToolUse hooks like Claude Code.
We need to intercept at the NTM level when sending commands to their panes.

## Implementation

### In ntm send command:
1. Before sending to pane, check if target is non-Claude agent
2. If DCG enabled, extract command portion from send payload
3. Run: dcg check --command "<extracted_command>"
4. If dcg returns non-zero, block send and return error
5. If dcg returns zero, proceed with normal send

### Challenges
- Identifying "command" content in arbitrary send payloads
- Some sends are just text, not commands
- Need heuristic: if starts with common command patterns, check DCG

### Heuristics for command detection
- Starts with: git, rm, mv, cp, chmod, chown, kubectl, terraform
- Contains: sudo, -rf, --force, --hard
- Looks like shell: contains |, &&, ||, ;

### Code Location
- internal/commands/send.go (add DCG check)
- internal/integrations/dcg/check.go (dcg invocation wrapper)

## Testing
- Send safe command to Codex pane, verify passes
- Send dangerous command to Codex pane, verify blocked

 {"id":"bd-1c9ib","title":"RCH Integration: Remote Compilation Helper for Build Offloading","description":"Integrate RCH (remote_compilation_helper) to offload compilation to remote workers.

## Project Location
/dp/remote_compilation_helper (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent workflows often trigger parallel builds:
- Agent 1: cargo build on project A
- Agent 2: go build on project B
- Agent 3: cargo test on project A

This causes:
- CPU saturation (all cores maxed)
- Thermal throttling
- Slow agent response times
- Failed builds due to resource contention

## What RCH Does

RCH transparently offloads compilation to remote workers:

### Architecture
1. PreToolUse hook intercepts cargo/go build commands
2. RCH client checks for available workers
3. If worker available, rsync project and run remotely
4. Stream output back to local terminal
5. Rsync artifacts back when done

### Key Features
- Transparent: no agent changes needed
- Deduplication: same build on same worker reuses cache
- Fallback: if no workers, run locally
- Worker fleet: multiple machines can serve as workers

### Key RCH Commands
```bash
rch status            # Show worker status
rch workers list      # List available workers
rch build cargo       # Explicit remote build
rch config            # Configure workers
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. PreToolUse Hook (Primary)
Configure hook for all agents to route builds through RCH.

### 2. Dashboard Widget
Show compilation status: local vs remote builds, worker utilization, time saved.

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-rch-status: Get RCH and worker status
- --robot-rch-workers: List workers

## Considerations
- Network latency: remote only wins for long builds (>10s)
- Security: workers must be trusted (code execution)
- Artifacts: some builds produce local-only files
- DCG: RCH commands should be whitelisted

## Acceptance Criteria

1. RCH integration module exists at internal/integrations/rch/
2. PreToolUse hook configuration for cargo/go build interception works
3. Dashboard widget shows local vs remote build stats
4. Robot mode commands return valid JSON with worker status
5. Fallback to local build works when no workers available
6. All child beads (bd-1j956, bd-64v8u, bd-1o1q1, bd-398bh, bd-3u84u, bd-1ghcp) completed

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/integrations/rch/rch_test.go
- Test worker detection, build routing, fallback logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] RCH Integration Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing RCH integration"
# Test robot-rch-status command
ntm --robot-rch-status | jq -e '.rch_installed \!= null' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX RCH status check passed"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. rch binary detection and version check
2. Worker availability monitoring
3. Build offloading transparent to agents
4. Session stats tracked
5. Config section [integrations.rch] works
6. Graceful fallback when rch missing
7. Unit tests for detection and offloading
8. E2E test for full RCH integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:06:45.277322518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:01.921527712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c9ib","depends_on_id":"bd-1k3ga","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:06:45.307206019Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cg8z","title":"CLI: Add --dry-run flag for plan preview","description":"## Purpose
Implement --sessions-per-type flag to configure how many tmux sessions to create per agent type (cc, cod, gmi).

## Implementation Details

```go
// Flag definition
var sessionsPerType int

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().IntVar(&sessionsPerType, "sessions-per-type", 3,
        "Number of tmux sessions per agent type (default: 3)")
}

// Validation in runSwarm
func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    logger := slog.Default()
    
    // Validate sessions-per-type
    if sessionsPerType < 1 {
        return fmt.Errorf("--sessions-per-type must be at least 1, got %d", sessionsPerType)
    }
    if sessionsPerType > 10 {
        logger.Warn("high sessions-per-type may impact performance",
            "value", sessionsPerType)
    }
    
    logger.Info("session configuration",
        "sessions_per_type", sessionsPerType)
    
    // Apply to config
    config := loadSwarmConfig(configPath)
    config.SessionsPerType = sessionsPerType
    
    // ... continue ...
}

// Effect on session naming
// With --sessions-per-type=3:
//   cc_agents_1, cc_agents_2, cc_agents_3
//   cod_agents_1, cod_agents_2, cod_agents_3
//   gmi_agents_1, gmi_agents_2, gmi_agents_3

// PanesPerSession is calculated as:
//   panesPerSession = ceil(totalAgentsOfType / sessionsPerType)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log configured value at startup
- Warn if unusually high value
- Log calculated panes-per-session based on this value

## Session Distribution Example
```
Total CC agents: 10, Sessions: 3
  cc_agents_1: 4 panes
  cc_agents_2: 3 panes  
  cc_agents_3: 3 panes
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default value of 3
- [ ] Minimum value of 1 enforced
- [ ] Warning for values > 10
- [ ] Affects session naming (cc_agents_N)
- [ ] Affects panes-per-session calculation
- [ ] Config file override support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:12.539788822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:39:05.645358087Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:39:05.645309365Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cg8z","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.571831163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cg8z","depends_on_id":"bd-qy5bn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:10.673640881Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cu1f","title":"Feature: Robot Probe Command (--robot-probe)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-probe=SESSION` command that actively tests whether panes are responsive.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the established robot mode patterns:
- **Primary flag**: `--robot-probe=SESSION` (not positional args)
- **Pane filter**: `--panes=N` or `--panes=1,2,3` (shared global flag)
- **All outputs**: JSON format

## Command Interface

```bash
# Check all agent panes in session
ntm --robot-probe=myproject

# Check specific panes
ntm --robot-probe=myproject --panes=4

# Use aggressive probing
ntm --robot-probe=myproject --panes=2,3 --probe-method=interrupt_test
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:17:00Z",
  "probes": [
    {
      "pane": 4,
      "responsive": true,
      "method": "keystroke_echo",
      "latency_ms": 45,
      "output_delta_lines": 0,
      "confidence": "high",
      "recommendation": "healthy",
      "reasoning": "Pane responded to probe within 45ms"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_probed": 1,
    "responsive": 1,
    "unresponsive": 0
  }
}
```

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--panes=N` | Filter to specific pane(s) | All agent panes |
| `--probe-method=M` | keystroke_echo, status_request, interrupt_test | keystroke_echo |
| `--probe-timeout=MS` | Wait time for response | 5000 |
| `--probe-aggressive` | Fall back to interrupt_test if echo fails | false |

## Probe Methods

1. **keystroke_echo** (default, safest): Send null byte, check cursor movement
2. **status_request**: Send agent-specific status query
3. **interrupt_test**: Send Ctrl-C, check response (may interrupt work)

## Algorithm

```
1. Capture current pane content (baseline)
2. Send probe input based on method
3. Wait probe-timeout milliseconds
4. Capture new pane content
5. Compare and score confidence
6. Return structured result
```

## Confidence Levels

- **high**: Clear response measured
- **medium**: Ambiguous response (minor change)
- **low**: No response but process active

## When to Use

- Before killing a "stuck" agent
- As part of --robot-diagnose workflow
- When timeout-based detection is ambiguous

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-probe=SESSION` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--panes=N` filter works with comma-separated pane IDs
- [ ] `--probe-method` accepts: keystroke_echo, status_request, interrupt_test
- [ ] `--probe-timeout` configurable (default 5000ms)
- [ ] `--probe-aggressive` falls back to interrupt_test on echo failure
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly (no extra fields)
- [ ] Confidence levels computed correctly (high/medium/low)
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial failure, 2 on complete failure
- [ ] Non-existent session returns error JSON (not crash)
- [ ] Non-existent pane returns error in probes array

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/cli/robot_probe_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all probe options
- [ ] Baseline capture logic (mock tmux)
- [ ] Confidence scoring algorithm
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing sessions/panes
- [ ] Timeout handling

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_probe.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PROBE] Robot Probe Command Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PROBE]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot probe E2E tests"

# Setup: Create test session with agent
TEST_SESSION="e2e_probe_$$"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Creating test session: $TEST_SESSION"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt

# Wait for agent initialization
sleep 5

# Test 1: Basic probe
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: Basic probe all panes"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-probe="$TEST_SESSION")
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".session") == "$TEST_SESSION" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".summary.total_probed") -ge 1 ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: Specific pane probe
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: Probe specific pane"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-probe="$TEST_SESSION" --panes=1)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".probes | length") -eq 1 ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Non-existent session
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Non-existent session error"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-probe="nonexistent_session_xyz" 2>&1) || true
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".error" 2>/dev/null) \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Cleanup
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Cleanup: Destroying test session"
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot probe E2E tests passed"
